text: Why was the first tunnel not completed?
zh: 为什么第一条隧道没有完工?

text: In 1858, a French engineer, Aime Thome de Gamond,
zh: 1858年,法国工程师艾梅·托姆·德·加蒙

text: arrived in England with a plan for a 21-mile tunnel under the English Channel.
zh: 带着一项计划来到英国,打算在英吉利海峡下修建一条21英里长的隧道。

text: He said that it would be possible to build a platform in the centre of the Channel.
zh: 他说,完全可以在海峡中央建一个平台。

text: This platform would serve as a port and a railway station.
zh: 这个平台既可以作港口,也可以作火车站。

text: The tunnel would be well-ventilated if tall chimneys were built above sea level.
zh: 如果在海平面以上建造高高的烟囱,隧道就能保持良好通风。

text: In 1860, a better plan was put forward by an Englishman, William Low.
zh: 1860年,英国人威廉·洛提出了一个更好的方案。

text: He suggested that a double railway-tunnel should be built.
zh: 他建议修建一条双线铁路隧道。

text: This would solve the problem of ventilation for if a train entered this tunnel, it would draw in fresh air behind it.
zh: 这样就能解决通风问题,因为一列火车驶入隧道时,会把新鲜空气吸入其中。

text: Forty-two years later a tunnel was actually begun.
zh: 四十二年后,一条隧道真的开工了。

text: If, at the time, the British had not feared invasion, it would have been completed.
zh: 如果当时英国人不担心遭到入侵,这条隧道本可以建成。

text: The world had to wait almost another 100 years for the Channel Tunnel.
zh: 此后,世界又等了将近一百年,才等到英吉利海峡隧道的诞生。

text: It was officially opened on March 7, 1994, finally connecting Britain to the European continent.
zh: 它于1994年3月7日正式通车,终于将英国与欧洲大陆连接了起来。
